name: Stages
summary: Scribe, Veridia, and CloudClinic Stage/interface surfaces that consume mediated Core, GOS, Session Runtime, AACI, and MSR state.
invariants: Stages are governed application consumers, not law engines.
Stages consume mediated surfaces and do not own Core law, GOS policy, or clinical authority.
Stage surfaces must avoid direct raw clinical data exposure and direct identifiers.
Native macOS UI remains scaffold/foundation maturity until explicitly implemented and validated.
allowedWork: Maintain Stage-safe mediated surfaces for Scribe, Veridia, and CloudClinic.
Add or repair smoke-testable Stage executable paths.
Align Stage docs with interface doctrine and macOS 26+ Liquid Glass baseline.
Validate Stage changes through Swift tests and Stage smoke commands.
forbiddenWork: Claim final UI delivery without implementation.
Expose raw direct identifiers or unmediated clinical payloads.
Give Stages clinical, regulatory, signature, prescription, referral, or finalization authority.
knownGaps: APP-011 is DONE for Veridia session path work.
APP-012 is BLOCKED until Core/GOS/runtime/Boundary/Custom readiness is satisfied.
Final Stage UI shells are not implemented.
notes: The territory id remains `apps` for compatibility, but the canonical HealthOS concept is Stage.
This territory covers Stage/interface boundaries, not product task completion.
APP-013 established Veridia as the canonical patient identity Stage name (2026-05-04). All future work uses Veridia naming.
ST-011 did not change APP-011 or APP-012 status; ADR-0013 later blocked APP-012 as Stage implementation.
